input_candidates.truth_value: Fe–Mn–Al alloys exhibit a combination of covalent and metallic bonding, which results in good electronic conductivity, relatively high melting points and high hardness.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Formation enthalpy and cohesive energy both increase monotonically with increasing Mn content across the Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl series; FeAl (x=0) possesses the lowest formation enthalpy (-0.391 eV·atom^{-1}) and is the most thermodynamically stable alloy.

input_candidates.truth_value: FeAl is the most stable alloy in the Fe1-xMnxAl system based on formation enthalpy and cohesive energy.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Chemical bonding analysis indicates that all Fe-Mn-Al alloys exhibit a combination of covalent and metallic bonds, leading to predicted good electronic conductivity, high melting points and high hardness.

input_candidates.truth_value: MnAl exhibits the largest elastic anisotropy among Fe–Mn–Al alloys (A^U = 3.80), while FeAl has the smallest (A^U = 0.56).
input_candidates.prediction_value: Brittle-to-ductile behavior predicted: FeAl and Fe_{0.875}Mn_{0.125}Al are brittle (B/G = 1.56 and 1.75, respectively), while all other compositions with higher Mn content are ductile (B/G > 1.75).

input_candidates.truth_value: Among mixed Fe-Mn-Al compounds, Fe0.5Mn0.5Al has the largest elastic anisotropy (A^U = 2.06).
input_candidates.prediction_value: MnAl exhibits the strongest elastic anisotropy among the studied alloys (universal anisotropic index A^U = 3.80), whereas FeAl is the most isotropic (A^U = 0.56).

input_candidates.truth_value: The Debye temperature decreases from FeAl to Fe0.5Mn0.5Al with increasing Mn content, then increases to Fe0.25Mn0.75Al before decreasing again to MnAl.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fe_{0.5}Mn_{0.5}Al shows anomalous extrema including the lowest Young's modulus (202.1 GPa), lowest shear modulus (77.2 GPa), lowest Debye temperature (533.3 K), and highest Poisson's ratio (0.309) and B/G ratio (2.28), indicating the highest ductility and compressibility among the intermediate compounds.
